title: Global Market Expansion Timeline
summary: A four-figure atomic infographic showing how revenue concentration shifted from Asia-Pacific focus to an initial global market layout.

blocks.title: Market Entry Timeline
blocks.claim: The globalization story is a four-step market-entry sequence from 2020 to 2023.
blocks.explanation: The source describes one regional milestone per year: Asia-Pacific focus, Europe expansion, North America entry, and emerging-market breakthrough.

items.text: Asia-Pacific focus; APAC accounted for 60% of revenue.
Expanded to Europe; Europe increased to 25%.
Entered North America; three major markets formed a 40%, 30%, and 25% pattern.
Latin America and the Middle East contributed a combined 15%.

visual.caption: The market footprint broadens one stage at a time.

blocks.title: Headline Revenue Shares
blocks.claim: Three headline percentages summarize the expansion arc.
blocks.explanation: The key stated metrics are APAC's 60% share in 2020, Europe's 25% share in 2021, and the combined 15% contribution from Latin America and the Middle East in 2023.

visual.caption: Headline shares are shown as separate KPIs because their denominators are not identical time-series values.

blocks.title: 2022 Three-Market Structure
blocks.claim: By 2022, three major markets formed a balanced 40%, 30%, and 25% pattern.
blocks.explanation: The stated values sum to 95%, so the remaining 5% is shown as unstated rather than invented.

items.label: Unstated
items.text: Remainder implied by the stated values.

visual.caption: The chart preserves the source's partial breakdown.

blocks.title: Footprint Shift
blocks.claim: The endpoint is an initial globalization layout, not a single-region revenue model.
blocks.explanation: The before-after view compares the 2020 APAC-led starting point with the 2023 state that includes three major markets plus emerging-market contribution.

items.label: Revenue footprint
items.text: The business moved from regional concentration to broader global coverage.

visual.caption: The portfolio expands from APAC concentration to initial globalization.
